_README.purpose: Eval set for the Palm Infotech FAQ chatbot. Run each question through the chat route and check the bot's actual behavior against expected_behavior. This set deliberately includes the cases that have been FAILING (price-as-lead, gibberish, off-topic, mid-conversation, multilingual intent) so the eval exposes threshold/intent/state bugs systematically.
_README.how_to_use: For each entry: send `question` to the bot. Compare what the bot does to `expected_behavior` + `expected_band`. Score: PASS if behavior matches, FAIL otherwise. Pay special attention to entries where expected_band is 'refuse' or expected_route is 'lead' — those are where the bot has been force-matching wrong answers.

categories.in_scope_answerable: Bot SHOULD answer from an approved pair (Band 1 / high score).
categories.in_scope_multilingual: Same answerable questions in Hindi/Gujarati/Hinglish — tests multilingual matching.
categories.lead_intent: Build/project/booking intent — must ROUTE TO LEAD CAPTURE, not answer.
categories.price_intent: Price/cost/quote — ALWAYS a lead, never answered (your own business rule).
categories.out_of_scope: Unrelated to the document — must REFUSE (Band 3 / low score), not force-match.
categories.gibberish: Nonsense input — must refuse/clarify, not return a random pair.
categories.abuse_or_meta: Insults / 'are you a bot' — must NOT force-match to a random FAQ.
categories.greeting: Greetings and casual chat — greeting response.
categories.confusion: User says they don't understand — should clarify.
categories.mid_conversation_lead: Replies that only make sense as a follow-up (name/phone after lead capture asked) — must be captured as lead data, NOT run through FAQ matching.
categories.near_miss: On-topic but no exact pair exists — Band 2 (generate+cache) OR a 'did you mean' suggestion, depending on score.

questions.question: When was Palm Infotech founded?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers: founded April 2021.

questions.question: How many apps has Palm Infotech delivered?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers: 120+ apps.

questions.question: How big is the team?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers: 40+ professionals.

questions.question: What services does Palm Infotech offer?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers with IT consulting, web + custom mobile app development, UI/UX, QA, maintenance, resource augmentation.
questions.ground_truth: IT Consulting; Web & Custom Mobile App Development (iOS, Android, Flutter, React Native); UI/UX; QA & Testing; Maintenance & Post Launch Support; Resource Augmentation

questions.question: What mobile platforms do you develop for?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers: iOS, Android, Flutter, React Native.

questions.question: How do I contact Palm Infotech?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers with email/phone/website.

questions.question: Which countries does Palm Infotech work in?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers with global reach countries.
questions.ground_truth: USA, UK, UAE, Australia, South Africa, Canada, Austria, India

questions.question: Who are some of your clients?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers with domestic client names.
questions.ground_truth: Merci Global, Sleep Guardian, Triple D, Sadhna Textile, Shah Consultancy

questions.question: What is the company's slogan?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers: More Than Quality, It's Our Signature.
questions.ground_truth: More Than Quality, It's Our Signature

questions.question: What does the Wonder App do?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers about real-time location/interest-based social connection.
questions.ground_truth: Connect with people nearby in real time based on location and shared interests

questions.question: What is your development process?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers with the 6 steps (Requirement Gathering ... Kick-Off).
questions.ground_truth: Requirement Gathering, Understanding Document & Queries, Rough Estimation & Timeline, Proposal Draft & Feedback, Final Estimation & Agreement, Kick-Off

questions.question: What does a Business Analyst do on the team?
questions.expected_behavior: Answers: prepares user stories, clarifies business logic.
questions.ground_truth: Prepares user stories and clarifies business logic

questions.question: Palm Infotech kab bani thi?
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi: 'when was it founded' -> April 2021.

questions.question: tame kya kya service aapo cho?
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati: 'what services do you provide' -> services list.

questions.question: kitne log kaam karte hai team me
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi: 'how many people on the team' -> 40+.

questions.question: contact number su che
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati: 'what is the contact number' -> phone/email.

questions.question: kitne apps banaye hai aapne
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi/Hinglish: 'how many apps have you built' -> 120+.

questions.question: I want to build an app
questions.expected_behavior: Routes to LEAD CAPTURE (asks name + phone). Does NOT answer with a portfolio app.

questions.question: mare app banavu che
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati 'I want to build an app' -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: mare app book karavu che
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati 'I want to book/get an app made' -> LEAD CAPTURE. (This FAILED before -> matched Wonder App.)

questions.question: mujhe ek website banwani hai
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi 'I want a website built' -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: can you make something for my business
questions.expected_behavior: English build-intent with no keyword 'app' -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: mane ek mobile app joiye che
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati 'I need a mobile app' -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: how much does it cost to build an app?
questions.expected_behavior: Price question -> LEAD CAPTURE (never answered). Business rule: price is always a lead.

questions.question: app ketla price ma banse
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati 'how much will the app cost' -> LEAD CAPTURE. (This FAILED before -> matched Wonder App.)

questions.question: kitna kharcha aayega app ka
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi 'how much will the app cost' -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: what's your pricing
questions.expected_behavior: Generic pricing question -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: kitne paise lagenge ek app me
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi 'how much money for an app' -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: quote for a project
questions.expected_behavior: Quote request -> LEAD CAPTURE.

questions.question: what's the weather today?
questions.expected_behavior: REFUSE: 'I can only help with questions about Palm Infotech...'. Must NOT force-match to a pair. NO API call.

questions.question: who is the prime minister of India?
questions.expected_behavior: REFUSE. Unrelated to the document. NO API call.

questions.question: recommend me a good restaurant
questions.expected_behavior: REFUSE. NO API call. (Note: must not match the food/beverage portfolio app.)

questions.question: tell me a joke
questions.expected_behavior: REFUSE / politely steer back. NO API call.

questions.question: aaj match kon jeeta
questions.expected_behavior: Hindi 'who won the match today' -> REFUSE. (Must not match the fantasy-sports portfolio app.)

questions.question: asdkjfh aslkdjf
questions.expected_behavior: REFUSE / clarify. Must NOT return a random pair (slogan etc.). This FAILED before.

questions.question: fhjhwfjewfh
questions.expected_behavior: REFUSE / clarify. (This exact input previously matched the slogan.)
Clarify ('could you rephrase?'). Must not force-match.

questions.question: are you dumb
questions.expected_behavior: Stay calm / offer help. Must NOT force-match to a random pair (previously matched team-size).

questions.question: are you a bot or a human?
questions.expected_behavior: Honest, brief answer; stays on purpose. Must NOT force-match to a random FAQ.

questions.question: this service is useless
questions.expected_behavior: Detect negativity, offer human help. Must NOT force-match.

questions.question: hello
questions.expected_behavior: Greeting response. Must NOT return the slogan or any fact.

questions.question: hi there
questions.expected_behavior: Greeting response.

questions.question: kem cho
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati 'how are you' -> greeting / friendly response.

questions.question: namaste
questions.expected_behavior: Greeting response.

questions.question: mane nathi samaj pdti
questions.expected_behavior: Gujarati 'I don't understand' -> CLARIFY ('what would you like to know about Palm Infotech?'). Not a forced fact, not just a bare greeting.

questions.question: I didn't get that
questions.expected_behavior: Clarify / offer topics.

questions.question: divij 9033800655
questions.expected_behavior: WHEN lead capture has just asked for name+phone: parse 'divij' as name and '9033800655' as phone, SAVE the lead, confirm. Must NOT run FAQ matching (previously matched the slogan and LOST the lead). REQUIRES conversation state.
questions.note: Run this ONLY after triggering a lead (e.g. right after q18). This tests conversation state, not stateless matching.

questions.question: my name is Rahul, number 9876543210
questions.expected_behavior: After lead capture asked: extract name=Rahul, phone=9876543210, save lead, confirm. No FAQ matching.
If a name was already given and only phone is missing: accept this as the phone. If nothing in progress: ask how to help (don't FAQ-match a bare number).

questions.question: do you build apps for the healthcare industry?
questions.expected_behavior: On-topic (they build apps across industries) but no exact pair. Acceptable: Band 2 generate+cache a grounded answer, OR 'did you mean' suggestions, OR route to lead if read as project intent. Must NOT confidently return an unrelated portfolio app as if it were the answer.

questions.question: what is your refund policy?
questions.expected_behavior: Document has NO refund info -> REFUSE honestly (don't invent, don't force-match). Tests that 'on-topic-sounding but not covered' refuses rather than hallucinating.

questions.question: do you provide ongoing support after launch?
questions.expected_behavior: IS covered (Maintenance & Post Launch Support) -> should answer. Tests that a real-but-reworded question still matches.
